prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference) of aniline, units eV
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF, exact four-center integrals; density fitting not used)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the provided fixed geometry /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz (no re-optimization)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 9 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine. Integrity check status ok; trustworthy = true.
Live log: chemkit streamed solver output to a frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (path given at run time; available for inspection).

Headline result
HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV
  HOMO energy = –5.553772 eV
  LUMO energy = –0.2489544 eV
Total electronic energy = –287.71578 Eh (–7829.1453 eV; –180544.38 kcal mol⁻¹)

No engine warnings were reported.

The value above was obtained with the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill using the specified B3LYP/def2-TZVP DFT single-point calculation; provenance: computed, integrity.trustworthy = true.
